title: Eddington
logline: During the COVID-19 pandemic in May 2020, a small-town New Mexico sheriff running for mayor against the incumbent becomes entangled in escalating violence when protests erupt and a double murder occurs, forcing him to cover up his own crimes while maintaining his campaign.
antagonistOrConflict: Joe's own moral corruption and the cascading consequences of his actions; externally, the political rivalry with Ted Garcia and the social unrest in Eddington
tone: Bleak, satirical, morally corrosive, and increasingly nightmarish; a COVID-era western that descends from political tension into violent chaos
themes: Moral decay and corruption of power
The erosion of truth in polarized America
The banality of evil in small-town politics

actBreaks.description: Joe hosts a disastrous family dinner that exposes his isolation and Louise's contempt, ending the setup phase before the murders occur.

validation.ruling: Scene #60 is a dinner party where Vernon notices Louise's disturbing artwork and the shrine to her dead father Richmond, creating deep tension about Joe's relationship to Richmond's death. This family gathering forces all the dysfunction and secrets into one room, fundamentally shifting the story from Joe's external law enforcement role to his internal family crisis—a major thematic and directional turn the story cannot recover from.

actBreaks.description: Michael escapes from jail and Joe's investigation collapses, propelling the story into its final violent spiral.

validation.ruling: Scene #122 shows Michael imprisoned in a tiny cell while Joe covertly monitors his phone call with an earpod, watched by Lakota. This represents Joe's complete transformation into a corrupt authority figure who has arrested an innocent man, crossed the final ethical line, and is now under surveillance himself—an irreversible plot twist that fundamentally changes the story's direction into its final act.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a three-act descent into hell, structured around Joe's political campaign that becomes a murder cover-up. Act One (scenes 1-60) establishes the COVID-fractured town, Joe's mayoral ambition against Ted Garcia, and the brewing protest movement—culminating in the family dinner that reveals Joe's personal failures. Act Two (scenes 61-122) begins with Joe committing the double murder of Ted and Eric, then desperately framing Michael while maintaining his campaign; this act is defined by Joe's increasingly frantic attempts to control the narrative. Act Three (scenes 123-168) explodes into chaos when Michael escapes, leading to Joe's rampage through town and his ultimate physical and moral destruction. The structure mirrors a classic western revenge tale inverted—the 'hero' is the villain, and his victory is his damnation.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately methodical in Act One, using short scenes (average 1.3 pages) to create a mosaic of small-town COVID life and political maneuvering. Act Two accelerates dramatically after the murders (scene 61), with interrogation scenes and investigative sequences building paranoid momentum. The final act (from scene 123 onward) becomes almost breathless—Joe's nighttime rampage through Eddington unfolds in rapid-fire scenes that rarely exceed a page, creating a fever-dream quality. Notable pacing shifts include the extended Town Hall sequence (scenes 64-74) that plays Joe's public humiliation in real-time, and the interrogation of Michael (scenes 109-122) which stretches tension through repetition and psychological pressure.

incitingIncident.description: Inside Garcia's bar, Ted Garcia pitches a Big Tech data center development to Phil and Warren, promising construction jobs and tax revenue with clawbacks, while Joe taps on the window outside then enters to remove Lodge from the premises.

validation.ruling: Scene #12 establishes the central conflict: Ted Garcia is conducting illegal 'essential business' meetings at his bar to push through a Big Tech data complex deal, directly challenging Joe's authority as sheriff when Joe confronts him about COVID violations. This shatters Joe's ability to maintain order and introduces the corrupt power dynamics that drive the entire story.

midpoint.description: Joe enters wearing two masks and a face shield; Lodge coughs at him repeatedly; Joe grabs Lodge's shirt and Lodge swings a vodka bottle onto Joe's head, shattering it; Joe shoots Lodge in the head.

validation.ruling: Scene #92 shows Joe gathering cleanup supplies after an event, but the true point of no return is Scene #91 where Joe shoots Lodge in the head after being attacked. This irreversible act of killing transforms Joe from reactive law enforcer to proactive criminal covering up murder, fundamentally shifting his role and raising personal stakes to maximum—he cannot return to being a legitimate sheriff after this.

climax.description: Joe enters the kitchen shirtless to find Louise holding two packed suitcases; she says she can't stay if it's not safe, and Joe tells her he's being punished; she asks if Vernon is responsible and whether Dawn could be involved, then Joe begs her not to leave.

validation.ruling: Scene #138 shows Brian trying to open his bedroom window, which is setup not climax. Scene #143 (INT. KITCHEN - SECONDS LATER, p130) is the peak confrontation where Joe faces the ultimate obstacle in his own kitchen—the direct, unavoidable confrontation that resolves the main story question and demonstrates his character arc completion at maximum dramatic tension.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lodge, a disheveled Black man in his late 40s, speaks directly to camera in a manic monologue about boxes (dwellings) and accuses unseen listeners of arrogance and godlessness.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT/PLAZA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lodge continues his monologue in front of ten square blocks of glowing stores, demanding whether the listener would work for him and accusing them of being godless.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lodge rants on a lamp-lit street in front of the Sheriff's Office, claiming he sees people's rot and promising sixty million dollars if they work for him while warning of devils.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark Zuckerberg steps out of a stretch limo holding a map and scrutinizes the area while Lodge narrates in voice-over about seeing a King Demon looking at boxes.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lodge stands before fenced-off private property marked for development and claims he can show people how to make hundreds of millions in seven days.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lodge delivers a final monologue about pigs and liars burning, then the camera pans to reveal Gil with his dog at the base of a cell phone tower as a car approaches with headlights.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOP OF THE HILL - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Gil leads Joe Cross up the hill to the cell tower where they discover Mitchell's charred corpse in a burned wheelchair at the tower's base, with a lighter in hand, gasoline canister nearby, and a smartphone on tripod aimed at the scene.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduces protagonist Joe Cross and establishes the inciting incident of Mitchell's apparent self-immolation at the cell tower

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe drives away furiously, turns on his dash camera to record a message about a near-miss in wrong jurisdiction, then receives a radio call about Lodge being a nuisance in town and takes the call.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe's police cruiser passes the highway sign for Eddington, NM (Population: 3,135) and continues toward the ten-square-block collection of adobe shops.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two teenage boys, Eric Garcia and Brian, stand by a fire hydrant on Eddington Strip passing a joint while Brian watches TikTok and Eric mocks progressive content.

scenes.heading: EXT. BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe approaches Garcia's bar where Lodge pulls mercilessly at the locked front door, shouting that the building is no temple and calling it a joke.

scenes.heading: INT. GARCIA’S - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Inside Garcia's bar, Ted Garcia pitches a Big Tech data center development to Phil and Warren, promising construction jobs and tax revenue with clawbacks, while Joe taps on the window outside then enters to remove Lodge from the premises.
scenes.turningPointNote: Establishes the central political conflict: Ted's Big Tech development deal and Joe's opposition, setting up their rivalry

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A framed portrait of deceased Sheriff Richmond hangs in Joe's foyer with a memorial garden beneath it while an older woman's voice off-screen rants about pandemic simulations and conspiracy theories.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe enters his bedroom to find Louise making a rag doll in bed; she reacts with distaste to his body, asks about the body call, becomes pale when he mentions the bar, and he notices a new embroidery of a girl's back stitched with scars.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe wakes alone to commotion from the kitchen where Dawn lectures Louise off-screen about government manipulation and Gitmo, and Joe grabs his inhaler and takes a puff.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Dawn lectures Louise in the kitchen about COVID conspiracy theories, DNA collection, and fluoride while Joe enters, sees an empty Haldol prescription bottle, and Louise doesn't answer when he asks if she's out of medication.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe drives past shuttered storefronts while listening to Tucker Carlson on his phone discuss government threats to seize and isolate children during the pandemic.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Sheriff's Office contains a barred prison cell, an Eddington Library section with bookshelves, and a framed photo of deceased Richmond on the wall; Michael and Guy work at their desks while an old masked man reads in the library chair.

scenes.heading: EXT. GROCERY STORE/PHARMACY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe stands in line outside the grocery store where an employee stops maskless Fred from entering; Ted calls out from the back of the line, the employee seizes Fred by the arm, and Joe intervenes telling the employee not to handle Fred that way.

scenes.heading: INT. GROCERY STORE/PHARMACY - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe enters the grocery store maskless to refill Louise's Haloperidol prescription while Fred is escorted out; the store owner asks Joe to wear a mask, a customer lectures him about herd immunity, Ted tries to intervene, and Joe corrects Ted to call him Sheriff before leaving.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe's public defiance of mask mandates and confrontation with Ted escalates their conflict and establishes Joe's anti-mandate stance

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe gets in his car feeling humiliated and proud, receives a Facebook notification that Fred tagged him in a photo thanking him, smiles, then pulls over and records an announcement video declaring he's running for mayor on an anti-mask platform.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe announces his mayoral candidacy, directly challenging Ted and committing to his anti-mask political platform

scenes.heading: INT. WARREN’S HOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Warren sits at his laptop during a Zoom meeting where Paula demands transparency about Ted's NDA and secret Tech deal; Warren receives a text from Michael with a link to Joe's announcement video and watches it.

scenes.heading: INT. TED’S OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Ted sits at his desk during the Zoom meeting as Paula accuses him of making decisions for personal profit; Phil kicks Paula off the call, then Warren texts Ted a link to Joe's announcement video with the message that they have a problem.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Joe enters the Sheriff's Office full of energy and instructs Michael to create a campaign sign in Photoshop with the slogan "Joe Cross for Mayor" while the phone starts ringing and Joe begins brainstorming slogans on a blackboard.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe steps outside to find Ted waiting; Ted apologizes if he offended Joe, but Joe says no apology is needed and explains he's been thinking about running for a long time with his own vision for the town.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe arrives home, pulls a handmade "Joe Cross for Mayor" sign from his trunk featuring the slogan "I CAN'T BREATHE" with a cartoon of a dead masked man, and plants it in front of his driveway.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe enters his house holding flowers and pharmacy goods to the sound of Louise and Dawn arguing off-screen about Joe's mayoral run, with Louise saying she can't have stress and can't slip again.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Dawn stands at Joe's locked bedroom door trying to soothe Louise while Joe peers in from around the corner and sees his announcement video playing on the laptop; Louise screams at Dawn about her father Richmond through the door.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe goes to his bedroom window where Louise sees him, rises, and faces the wall; she accuses him of watching for her to slip and pushing her off a cliff while he denies it and asks her to stop referring to herself as "her."

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Eric drives past Joe's house with Brian as passenger, slowing down to observe Joe talking to the window and Dawn spying on the conversation from the other side of the house.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT - EVENING
scenes.summary: At an outdoor parking lot party in the desert, Eric and Brian arrive and tease Michael who stands apart watching; Brian ogles a progressive girl holding an Angela Davis book, Eric warns she's annoyingly woke, but Brian insists he loves her and plans to DM her.

scenes.heading: EXT. TED’S HOUSE - PORCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ted and Warren sit masked on Ted's porch discussing strategy; Warren suggests threatening Joe about his wife's issues, but Ted refuses to go there; Eric's car arrives and parks in the background.

scenes.heading: INT. ERIC’S ROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Ted enters Eric's room and confronts him about going to a gathering; Eric lies about being distanced, Ted darkly asks what Eric just called him when Eric says "bro," then Ted notices a handwritten letter on Eric's desk for someone's birthday.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIAN’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brian lies in bed scrolling Twitter, liking meme posts and laughing at a 2020 joke, then scrolls past an article about police killing George Floyd without engaging with it.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe slips campaign fliers under shop doors on Main Street at night.

scenes.heading: INT. CROSS HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe comes home to a dark empty house, finds his bedroom door locked, calls for Louise and Dawn but gets no answer, and wanders confused.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe wakes on the fold-out couch as Louise and Dawn return home; Louise avoids eye contact and finds the bedroom locked, Dawn unlocks it with a key saying Joe forfeited his right to the bed, and Louise reveals Dawn needed her to drive somewhere overnight.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe jogs on a road while dictating a response letter in voice-over about New Mexico's COVID response, passing another jogger wearing a face mask.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe does sit-ups, showers vigorously, and trims his beard while continuing to dictate his response letter in voice-over criticizing anti-scientific bluster.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe pops his head into the bedroom where Louise lies reading a QAnon Reddit thread on her phone; he asks her to have dinner together at 7:30, she reluctantly agrees after he offers to cook.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANTIQUE/HARDWARE STORE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe stands outside a hardware/antique store with a racist lawn jockey in the window, offering his campaign sign to the owner who shakes his head no while Joe continues dictating his letter in voice-over.

scenes.heading: EXT. GUN STORE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe hangs his campaign sign in the window of a gun store while dictating in voice-over that the science on COVID is disputed worldwide.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSES - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe introduces himself as a mayoral candidate to different people on their lawns while maintaining distance; one homeowner enthusiastically signs his petition and Joe dictates about Trump taking hydroxychloroquine.

scenes.heading: INT. GROCERY STORE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe shops for expensive gourmet dinner ingredients, shakes hands with the masked cashier, introduces himself as a candidate to another worker, and declares he will not police face masks before leaving.

scenes.heading: INT. JUANITA’S RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe talks to masked restaurant owner Paula at Juanita's while dictating in voice-over about condemning businesses to death in the name of unscientific policies.

scenes.heading: EXT. JUANITA’S COURTYARD - LATER
scenes.summary: Joe sits with Paula in Juanita's courtyard as she reveals her suspicions that Ted is getting kickbacks from the data center deal and saving a mystery company billions while locals suffer.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paula provides Joe with ammunition against Ted by alleging corruption and kickbacks in the Big Tech deal

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE CAR (MOVING) - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe drives down a highway toward Taos, dictating aloud to his phone recorder about masks being declared useless against the virus.

scenes.heading: INT. KINKO’S (TAOS) - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe talks up the Kinko's owner while Michael gathers new campaign signs with anti-Big Tech and anti-corruption slogans targeting Ted Garcia, as Joe dictates about the Radical Left banning miracle cures.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE CRUISER - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. COURTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CRUISER (MOVING) - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Joe chases Eric in his cruiser but brakes to avoid hitting protesters gathered in the street; he exits the vehicle and approaches them while Brian films him.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Eric hides in an alley, discovers Sarah accepted his friend request on Instagram, likes her posts about George Floyd, and finds a flyer for a Black Lives Matter event she's attending.

scenes.heading: INT. CITY HALL - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Joe arrives at City Hall where Guy is painting a sign and asks Michael to disperse the assembly if they lack a permit; Guy offers to go but Joe insists Michael handle it.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Michael approaches the protest and asks Sarah for a permit; she refuses, arguing they don't need police permission to protest police violence, and challenges Michael about his feelings on the incident.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIAN’S HOUSE - DINING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Brian explains the eight white identities to his parents at dinner; his father responds by asking if he's retarded and pointing out that Brian is white.

scenes.heading: INT. CROSS HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Joe arrives home at 6:40, calls for Lou and Dawn but receives no response, then smiles and begins preparing groceries in the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S KITCHEN - EVENING
scenes.summary: Joe chops vegetables while on a Zoom call with Guy, who plays audio of protesters and discusses riots and Antifa activity; Joe dismisses the Minneapolis incident as unrelated to them.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BATHROOM - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Joe trims his pubic hair over the toilet while Guy continues discussing Antifa militants and explosives on the Zoom call; the laptop faces the wall during this.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe tries on shirts while Guy gives commentary on a video; the smoke alarm goes off and Joe runs to attend to it as the clock shows 7:02, then later 7:28.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Everyone eats Joe's pasta at the kitchen table; Vernon admires Louise's dolls and embroidery, calling her a compulsive artist, then notices a photo of Richmond and asks about him.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe watches from the window as Louise hugs Vernon and the others goodbye outside the house.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe asks Louise why she took the baby; she says she was taking her to the police, thinking she was lost; Joe reveals he saw the security footage and defends not arresting her.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe wakes up alone in bed.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe finds Dawn watching a YouTube video about COVID being a manmade virus; he asks where Louise went but Dawn ignores him.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - MORNING
scenes.summary: Guy drives a sign into the sidewalk advertising Joe's Town Hall meeting at Juanita's with free food; Michael displays the sign in the sheriff's office window.

scenes.heading: EXT. JUANITA’S RESTAURANT - LATE MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe records a promotional video outside Juanita's with his karaoke microphone, advertising the Town Hall meeting to people waiting for breakfast burritos while Michael and Paula set up in the courtyard.

scenes.heading: INT. GROCERY STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe shops for Town Hall supplies wearing a sign when he sees people gathered around a TV showing news footage of a masked driver in a black pickup truck crashing into a church.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe speeds from the market on the phone with Paula, notices his campaign sign replaced by Ted Garcia's, then a boy with an 'I CANT BREATH' sign jumps in front of his car before running away.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe arrives at the office where Guy wears riot gear in a panic; Michael answers the phone while Joe changes into his ceremonial regalia as the TV shows Santa Fe protest coverage.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe walks toward the protest in his regalia followed by the irate woman and Michael; Paula intercepts him about a picture but Joe insists it's a fake setup.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe turns the corner to find the protest has grown larger with Lodge coughing among them; an old man and armed woman confront protesters; Joe spots Eric holding a blown-up photo of him wrestling John.

scenes.heading: INT. JUANITA’S RESTAURANT - KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe enters Juanita's kitchen through the back door, drenched in sweat, while a Mexican woman preparing food watches him pass through.

scenes.heading: EXT. JUANITA’S RESTUARANT - COURTYARD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe surveys the courtyard from a corner and sees only eight people in the audience; he tells Guy he can't go out there and Guy nods unhelpfully.

scenes.heading: EXT. JUANITA’S RESTAURANT - COURTYARD - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe addresses the meager Town Hall crowd, claiming he has evidence Ted plans to replace local shops with server farms and accusing Ted of profiting from the shutdown; he promises to reopen Eddington.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe drives home in his regalia, negotiating with himself about whether to be excited or worried, then avoids the marching protest by taking a side street.

scenes.heading: EXT. RESIDENTIAL STREET - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brian marches with protesters when he receives a text from Eric showing a photo of Eric kissing Sarah in the car; Brian recognizes the location in the background and zooms in on it.

scenes.heading: EXT. CROSS HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe pulls up to his house and Louise runs out screaming and hitting him, calling him a bastard; Dawn follows with a duffel bag as Louise attacks Joe.
scenes.turningPointNote: Louise's violent confrontation with Joe after his Town Hall revelation marks a major rupture in their relationship and Joe's domestic life.

scenes.heading: INT. CROSS HOUSE - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Dawn shows Joe that Louise turned Richmond's portrait to face the wall and hid the rest of her doll collection from the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe takes three sleeping pills and lies in bed, then hears Dawn outside shaming Louise for hiding another photo of her father in the bushes.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brian drives through the desert listening to a podcast about white progressives and unconscious racism, then shuts off his headlights overlooking the parking lot where Eric's car is parked alone.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brian sneaks up to Eric's car and peers through the window to see Eric and Sarah having sex; he looks heartbroken then raises his phone to record video.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Michael stands outside holding a rifle and fires at a target sheet 100 feet away; the first shot misses.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHAEL’S APARTMENT - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Michael pins the target sheet with a bullet hole in the head to his wall, then receives an Instagram message request containing Brian's video of Sarah and Eric having sex; tears immediately fill Michael's eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Joe wakes alone at 4:16 PM to dozens of angry texts about the protest and property damage; he opens Facebook and watches a local news clip of Louise being interviewed.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Dawn paces outside on the phone, furiously telling Louise she erased Joe's one honorable act and failed herself by removing accountability; Joe watches from the back door then receives a call from Guy.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CRUISER (MOVING) - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Joe drives down the quiet Main Street, passing Ted's bar and the boarded-up antique store with its shattered window.

scenes.heading: EXT. TED’S HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Joe arrives at Ted's outdoor party with 50 people, double-parks with gumball lights flashing, and confronts Ted about the antique store window; Ted denies responsibility and they argue about the protest and Joe's mayoral campaign.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CRUISER (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe drives down Main Street and sees teens with signs watching something inside Ted's bar; when they spot his cruiser they run, dropping a sign; Joe discovers a smashed window at Ted's bar and approaches with his gun drawn.

scenes.heading: INT. TED’S BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lodge stands behind the bar coughing violently, holding vodka and whiskey bottles; Joe enters with his gun but pauses when he realizes Lodge is sick; Lodge drinks from both bottles and smells them, unable to tell the difference.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe retrieves two face masks from his glove compartment and puts both on, then throws a brick through the hardware store window to access a face shield.

scenes.heading: INT. TED’S BAR - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe enters wearing two masks and a face shield; Lodge coughs at him repeatedly; Joe grabs Lodge's shirt and Lodge swings a vodka bottle onto Joe's head, shattering it; Joe shoots Lodge in the head.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe kills Lodge, crossing the line from law enforcement into murder and beginning his descent into violent criminality.

scenes.heading: INT. ANTIQUE/HARDWARE STORE - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe enters the hardware store through the smashed window and gathers a shovel, trash bags, cleaning solution, bleach, mop, and a disposable protective coverall safety suit.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK ALLEY - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe pulls his car into the alley behind Ted's bar wearing a hazmat suit, then drags Lodge's corpse out and places it in his trash-bag-lined trunk.

scenes.heading: INT. TED’S BAR - LATER
scenes.summary: Joe mops up all the blood inside Ted's bar.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe digs a hole in the forest wearing his hazmat suit, then drags Lodge's body from his car and pushes it into the grave.

scenes.heading: INT. TED’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ted relaxes in his living room at the end of the party when he notices a purse on the coffee table; he confronts Eric about having a girl in the house against their agreement; a gunshot suddenly kills Ted and Eric is also shot.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ted and Eric are shot, escalating the violence to a double homicide and eliminating Joe's political rival.

scenes.heading: EXT. HILL - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Joe rises from the hill with a scoped rifle still wearing the coverall safety suit, surveys the scene below, then starts down toward the house with a rattling sound accompanying each step.

scenes.heading: INT. TED’S LIVING ROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe walks through the obliterated sliding-glass door, observes the room, steps over Eric's corpse while avoiding his expanding pool of blood, and peers down the hall.

scenes.heading: INT. TED’S ROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe enters Ted's bedroom, searches the closet, then finds a wedding ring at the desk with an inscription and pockets it.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe returns to the living room with an aerosol spray paint can, switches it to his left hand to disguise his handwriting, and spray-paints 'NO JU' on the wall.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE’S SHED - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe stuffs his bloody sheriff's uniform and safety suit into a BBQ grill, douses them with lighter fluid, and sets them on fire.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe enters the house in his underwear to find Dawn waiting in the dark; she tells him Louise isn't answering her calls, and Joe claims their clothes were contaminated by protocol before retreating to the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe wakes to panicked texts and missed calls from multiple people; he tries calling Louise but it goes to voicemail, then answers a call from Guy on speakerphone.

scenes.heading: EXT. TED’S HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe arrives at Ted's house wearing a mask to find Michael, Brian, and upset townspeople waiting; the Antique Store Owner demands to know what's going on, and Guy questions Joe about the mask.

scenes.heading: INT. TED’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe and Guy examine Ted and Eric's corpses; Joe sees Lakota on the hill and learns the shooter fired from Reservation land, meaning Lakota has jurisdiction over the crime scene.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jurisdictional conflict emerges as the murder scene crosses into Reservation territory, forcing Joe to share the investigation with Lakota and complicating his ability to control the narrative.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE’S CRUISER - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe covertly swaps his shoes for old muddy boots in his cruiser before Lakota approaches; when asked if he found something, Joe claims it's not there, and deflects Lakota's question about Reservation suspects.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR (MOVING) - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe drives away from Ted's property with Guy as passenger, passing Brian who stands with his back turned; Guy suggests ramming Lakota's following cruiser, then tells Joe he's glad Ted is dead because Ted was planning to fire Joe.

scenes.heading: EXT. SARAH’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe and Guy stand on a porch while Lakota watches from his parked car across the street; a middle-aged woman answers the door and Guy asks if her daughter is Sarah Allen.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - INTERROGATION ROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Joe and Guy interrogate Sarah in the sheriff's office; Joe aggressively questions her about Far Left sympathies and accuses her of involvement in the murders, while Sarah insists the GOP is planting fake protesters and that Eric wasn't her boyfriend.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Sarah's mother waits nervously in the sheriff's office with Dwight while Guy's interrogation of Sarah continues off-screen, escalating into shouting about white people, Martial Law, and planted violence.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Joe gives a press conference flanked by Michael and Guy, announcing Ted and Eric's murders and warning criminals they will face justice; he encourages Eddington residents to use their guns against anyone bringing violence into neighborhoods.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dawn watches Joe's press conference on TV from the couch, her eyes buzzing with worry as Joe warns that armed Eddington residents will defend their neighborhoods.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE JET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A private luxury plane courses over desert terrain at night while a news anchor's voice continues discussing the deployment of unarmed social workers and violence prevention coordinators.

scenes.heading: INT. PRIVATE JET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Inside the private jet, two men in Hawaiian shirts sit watching TV; one has a neck tattoo and the other has a muscular, sunbaked arm resting on the armrest, his face covered by a mask.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe sits on his bed in his underwear, fingering Ted's wedding ring while studying a notepad listing suspects including Sarah, Ted's assistant, Eric's friend, John, and Dawn; he writes then crosses out Dawn as a suspect.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S KITCHEN - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Dawn tells Joe that Louise's phone isn't ringing anymore and Vernon may have blocked her; Joe says he's been trying Louise too without success, and Dawn worries whether Louise blocked them or something happened to her.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE’S HOUSE - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe emerges from his house to find Lakota questioning his neighbor; Joe confronts Lakota about investigating on his side of town, and they argue about jurisdiction and whether Joe meant "my people" racially.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe enters the sheriff's office to find Brian sitting teary-eyed at Guy's desk while the Antique Store Owner waits in the corner; Guy tells Joe that Brian will only talk to him and has been avoiding eye contact.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Brian tells Joe he went to Eric's house the morning after sending a video to Michael and found police there; he reveals Michael attended their parties and stared at Eric and Sarah strangely, making Brian suspect Michael might be involved.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Guy urgently tells Joe they need to search Michael's place immediately before he can destroy evidence; when Joe questions Michael's motive, Guy whispers intensely that Michael could be working with the Reservation.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe asks to see Michael's phone and Instagram; Michael hesitantly shows him a video message of Eric and Sarah kissing that was already opened; Joe and Guy confront Michael about not showing them this evidence, then arrest him despite his protests and Lakota's objections.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe arrests Michael based on flimsy evidence, crossing a line by imprisoning someone he knows may be innocent while Lakota watches, escalating the jurisdictional and racial tensions.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Michael is locked in a tiny prison cell and begs for his phone call; Guy mocks him while Joe brings him a cordless phone, aware that Lakota is watching his every move from the front door.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CRUISER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe drives with an earpod in his ear listening to ringing, following Lakota's cruiser at a distance with headlights off; he tries calling Louise but it goes to voicemail, then notices a vigil at City Hall with Warren at the microphone.

scenes.heading: INT. CITY HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Warren leads a candlelight vigil for Ted and Eric at City Hall, speaking about progress and leadership; Brian gives a speech about Eric being his best friend and Ted beating cancer, but the crowd jeers him and someone throws a candle at his head.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe drives past the thinning vigil with Lakota still following on his tail.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe frantically parks and runs to the sheriff's office where the dumpster has been set on fire; Guy waits outside distraught, claiming he was only gone two seconds.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe enters to find Michael's cell empty with the key still in the lock; Guy insists he was only gone one minute to deal with the fire, and Joe discovers the fire extinguisher has already been used on a previous fire.
scenes.turningPointNote: Michael escapes from custody while Guy was distracted, creating a crisis that threatens to expose Joe's questionable arrest and further destabilize the investigation.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe runs to his cruiser and sees Brian standing at the end of the street anxiously watching; when Joe calls out to him, Brian backs away and flees.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CRUISER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe drives away while seeing Lakota extinguishing the fire with an extinguisher in his rearview mirror; Guy's car races ahead, and Joe turns onto Main Street where he becomes hypnotized by panic.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHAEL’S APARTMENT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Guy kicks open the door to Michael's dark apartment with his gun drawn and searches room to room, finding it empty; he notices a target sheet on the wall, then runs to the balcony and bangs on the neighbor's door to search their apartment too.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Guy returns to his car and speeds off from the apartment building.

scenes.heading: INT. GUY’S CRUISER (MOVING) - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Guy drives using his torchlight to search dark alleys and desert voids; he drives up a hill and from the top sees a sizable fire burning in the desert, then accelerates toward it.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CRUISER - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe drives down the highway using his torch to search dark spaces; he sees Guy's parked cruiser in the distance with Guy standing ahead of it, and pulls off to drive toward him.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe arrives to find Guy aiming his gun at Michael who stands terrified in the desert claiming he's surrounded by things in a circle; Guy steps forward and explodes into bloody pieces from a landmine, blowing Joe backward as chunks of Guy rain down.
scenes.turningPointNote: Guy is killed by a landmine in a devastating explosion that marks the story's descent into full violent chaos and reveals the scale of the trap set for law enforcement.

scenes.heading: INT. CRUISER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe climbs into Guy's cruiser, reverses away from the fire, then speeds toward an intersection to intercept a fleeing truck; he T-bones the truck, causing it to flip and eject two masked men before crashing and exploding into flames.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe finds a smartphone with its flashlight still on in the arroyo; he discovers it was recording video and reviews the library, finding footage of a masked man setting the dumpster fire and throwing a burning Bible into the farmhouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe walks limping through the night, gripping the masked man's smartphone in one hand and his gun in the other.

scenes.heading: EXT. BRIAN’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brian tries to sneak into his bedroom through the window from outside, but the window won't budge despite multiple attempts.

scenes.heading: EXT. BRIAN’S HOUSE - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Brian stands before his front door working up the nerve to enter, then hesitates before opening it.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIAN’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brian enters to find his mother waiting in the hall and his father at the kitchen table; his parents berate him for attending the protest and vigil, with his mother accusing him of killing his best friend and calling him a stupid child who brought violence to their town.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe walks toward his house covered in blood and dirt, seeing Vernon's truck and a vision of Louise, Dawn, and himself arguing; his phone vibrates with a call from Louise, and he answers to hear gunfire and Louise screaming before the line goes dead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe receives a call from Louise that ends with gunfire and her screams, suggesting she's been attacked or killed and pushing Joe past any remaining moral boundaries.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE’S HOUSE - BACK YARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe, now shirtless, stuffs his uniform shirt into a BBQ pit and sets it on fire while Louise watches him through the kitchen window with worried eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe enters the kitchen shirtless to find Louise holding two packed suitcases; she says she can't stay if it's not safe, and Joe tells her he's being punished; she asks if Vernon is responsible and whether Dawn could be involved, then Joe begs her not to leave.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe stumbles out and runs to a taxi cab whose driver has been shot dead through the windshield; he pulls out the corpse, jumps in, and drives away as rifle fire strikes the car; a tire blows out causing him to crash into a tree, then he flees on foot as more shots shatter the windows.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe's house is attacked by snipers who kill the taxi driver and nearly kill Joe, forcing him to flee on foot and marking the complete collapse of any safety or order in Eddington.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brian walks along the ghostly Main Street, slows at the taped-off police station, then continues to a bench where he wipes it down and lies down to sleep; a panting sound gets his attention and he sits up to see a bloody, limping Joe emerging from the darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. PHARMACY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe shoots through the pharmacy's glass door and enters delirious with one side of his face caked in blood; he guzzles water, pours rubbing alcohol on his head while screaming, then prepares to stitch his own head wound with needle and thread.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. PHARMACY - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. GUN SHOP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Bullets tear through an apartment bedroom wall and strike a sleeping person in bed, blasting them apart.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Joe spins while firing and unknowingly shoots Lakota in the leg and ankle, blowing off his foot, then runs to him horrified as distant gunshots ring out and a bullet strikes nearby.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe accidentally shoots his own ally Lakota in catastrophic friendly fire, marking the complete breakdown of control and the irreversible descent into chaos

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe enters a house and a 65-year-old man fires a shotgun that destroys the doorframe and peppers Joe's face; the man apologizes upon recognizing Joe, but Joe panics, picks up his machine gun, and runs past him, the gun's width brutally propelling the old man onto his back.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe runs out onto the porch, trips down the steps, crawls to retrieve his gun, then backpedals while having an asthma attack, sees someone running in the distance, fires the machine gun riddling a house with bullets until the gun clicks empty, then throws it and panics feeling utterly exposed in the desert.

scenes.heading: EXT. AMBULANCE (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe sits in the back of a moving ambulance while a paramedic tends to his head wound, both wearing face masks.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe is wheeled to a hospital where a guard reads his temperature as 101 and turns him away; the scene fades to black, then a TikTok video plays showing Brian six months later on a road trip from Jacksonville with his girlfriend, driving to Eddington.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major time jump of six months forward, showing Brian's new life and his return to Eddington, signaling a shift into the story's final phase

scenes.heading: EXT. SHERIFF’S OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: An objective wide view shows Brian being greeted by Dawn and Joe while his girlfriend films, as Dawn's voice plays over a speaker discussing conspiracy theories about Jonas Salk, Bill Gates, and the polio vaccine.

scenes.heading: INT. MAYOR’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe sits behind a desk in the mayor's office with Dawn beside him and framed news articles behind them about his not-guilty verdict and lawsuit threats, while Dawn pitches Brian on joining a tech initiative as his girlfriend films.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe is now mayor and was acquitted of all charges, representing a complete reversal of his legal jeopardy and consolidation of power

scenes.heading: INT. RESTROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Brian uses the restroom and listens to a voicemail from his mother begging him to come home, saying they were wrong, his father can't get out of bed, and they're proud of him and want him back in their lives.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAYOR’S OFFICE - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At the end of the day, Dawn wheels Joe out of the mayor's office building toward a wheelchair-accessible van.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Dawn drives the van up a dirt hill to a massive, lavish house that is one-third built with masked workers finishing their day.

scenes.heading: INT. MASSIVE HOUSE - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Dawn opens the door and wheels Joe inside their new massive house.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - EVENING
scenes.summary: In the large renovated kitchen, Dawn has positioned Joe in front of her laptop where they watch Brian's TikTok video about vaccine dangers, which already has over 3,000 likes.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dawn feeds Joe his dinner while taking bites of her own as a TV plays in the background.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Seen from a respectful distance down the hall, Dawn helps Joe use the toilet through the open bathroom door.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Dawn gives Joe a bath while recounting an anecdote from her day.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe sits in his wheelchair watching Young Mr. Lincoln on a 90-inch TV while Dawn browses Reddit on her laptop, gasps when she discovers an anti-government group called The Knighthood using one of Louise's dolls as their mascot, then desperately searches until finding an invitation to a rally.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dawn discovers Louise's doll has become the symbol of an anti-government extremist group, connecting her daughter's legacy to a dangerous movement and setting up the final confrontation

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dawn uses hospital-grade equipment to place Joe into their king-size bed, then climbs in beside him, turns off the lamp, curls up next to him with her hand on his chest, and says goodnight.
